title: Context-Enriched Natural Language Descriptions of Vessel Trajectories
summary: Researchers developed a framework that transforms raw vessel tracking data into human-readable natural language descriptions enriched with geographic and weather context. The method segments AIS trajectories into mobility-annotated episodes and leverages LLMs to generate descriptions, enabling better maritime analytics and reasoning.
content:
arXiv:2603.12287v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We address the problem of transforming raw vessel trajectory data collected from AIS into structured and semantically enriched representations interpretable by humans and directly usable by machine reasoning systems. We propose a context-aware trajectory abstraction framework that segments noisy AIS sequences into distinct trips each consisting of clean, mobility-annotated episodes. Each episode is further enriched with multi-source contextual information, such as nearby geographic entities, offshore navigation features, and weather conditions. Crucially, such representations can support generation of controlled natural language descriptions using LLMs. We empirically examine the quality of such descriptions generated using several LLMs over AIS data along with open contextual features. By increasing semantic density and reducing spatiotemporal complexity, this abstraction can facilitate downstream analytics and enable integration with LLMs for higher-level maritime reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 上下文丰富的船舶轨迹自然语言描述
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一个框架，将原始船舶跟踪数据转化为可读的自然语言描述，并用地理和天气背景信息丰富。该方法将AIS轨迹分割为带有运动注释的事件，并利用大语言模型生成描述，使更好的海事分析和推理成为可能。

fr.title: Descriptions en langage naturel enrichies en contexte des trajectoires de navires
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé un cadre qui transforme les données brutes de suivi des navires en descriptions en langage naturel lisibles enrichies avec du contexte géographique et météorologique. La méthode segmente les trajectoires AIS en épisodes annotés par la mobilité et exploite les LLM pour générer des descriptions, permettant une meilleure analyse et un meilleur raisonnement maritime.

de.title: Kontextbereicherte natürlichsprachliche Beschreibungen von Schiffstrajektorien
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten ein Framework, das rohe Schiffsüberwachungsdaten in lesbaren natürlichsprachlichen Beschreibungen umwandelt, die mit geografischen und meteorologischen Kontexten angereichert sind. Die Methode unterteilt AIS-Trajektorien in mobilitätsannotierte Episoden und nutzt LLMs zur Generierung von Beschreibungen, was bessere maritime Analytik und Überlegungen ermöglicht.

es.title: Descripciones en lenguaje natural enriquecidas en contexto de trayectorias de buques
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron un marco que transforma datos brutos de seguimiento de buques en descripciones legibles en lenguaje natural enriquecidas con contexto geográfico y meteorológico. El método segmenta trayectorias AIS en episodios anotados por movilidad y aprovecha LLMs para generar descripciones, permitiendo mejores análisis y razonamiento marítimo.

title: Efficient Reasoning with Balanced Thinking
summary: A training-free framework called ReBalance addresses inefficiencies in large reasoning models by identifying and correcting both overthinking and underthinking through confidence analysis. The approach improves accuracy while reducing computational redundancy across math, question-answering, and coding tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.12372v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) have shown remarkable reasoning capabilities, yet they often suffer from overthinking, expending redundant computational steps on simple problems, or underthinking, failing to explore sufficient reasoning paths despite inherent capabilities. These issues lead to inefficiencies and potential inaccuracies, limiting practical deployment in resource-constrained settings. Existing methods to mitigate overthinking, such as suppressing reflective keywords or adjusting reasoning length, may inadvertently induce underthinking, compromising accuracy. Therefore, we propose ReBalance, a training-free framework that achieves efficient reasoning with balanced thinking. ReBalance leverages confidence as a continuous indicator of reasoning dynamics, identifying overthinking through high confidence variance and underthinking via consistent overconfidence. By aggregating hidden states from a small-scale dataset into reasoning mode prototypes, we compute a steering vector to guide LRMs' reasoning trajectories. A dynamic control function modulates this vector's strength and direction based on real-time confidence, pruning redundancy during overthinking, and promoting exploration during underthinking. Extensive experiments conducted on four models ranging from 0.5B to 32B, and across nine benchmarks in math reasoning, general question answering, and coding tasks demonstrate that ReBalance effectively reduces output redundancy while improving accuracy, offering a general, training-free, and plug-and-play strategy for efficient and robust LRM deployment. Code is available at https://github.com/yu-lin-li/ReBalance .

zh.title: 高效推理与平衡思维
zh.summary: 一个名为ReBalance的免训练框架通过置信度分析来识别和纠正大型推理模型中的过度思考和不足思考，解决了这些模型的低效问题。该方法在数学、问答和编码任务中提高了准确性，同时减少了计算冗余。

fr.title: Raisonnement efficace avec pensée équilibrée
fr.summary: Un cadre sans entraînement appelé ReBalance aborde les inefficacités dans les grands modèles de raisonnement en identifiant et en corrigeant à la fois la surréflexion et la sous-réflexion par analyse de confiance. L'approche améliore la précision tout en réduisant la redondance informatique dans les tâches mathématiques, de questions-réponses et de codage.

de.title: Effiziente Argumentation mit ausgewogenem Denken
de.summary: Ein trainingsfreies Framework namens ReBalance behebt Ineffizienzen in großen Argumentationsmodellen, indem es Überdenken und Unterdenken durch Konfidenzanalyse identifiziert und korrigiert. Der Ansatz verbessert die Genauigkeit, während er Rechnerredundanz bei mathematischen, Frage-Antwort- und Codierungsaufgaben reduziert.

es.title: Razonamiento eficiente con pensamiento equilibrado
es.summary: Un marco sin entrenamiento llamado ReBalance aborda ineficiencias en grandes modelos de razonamiento mediante la identificación y corrección tanto del exceso de reflexión como de la falta de reflexión a través del análisis de confianza. El enfoque mejora la precisión mientras reduce la redundancia computacional en tareas matemáticas, de preguntas y respuestas, y de codificación.

title: Generating Expressive and Customizable Evals for Timeseries Data Analysis Agents with AgentFuel
summary: AgentFuel is a new tool that enables domain experts to create customized evaluation benchmarks for conversational data analysis agents handling timeseries data. The framework addresses gaps in existing evaluations by focusing on stateful and incident-specific queries across IoT, observability, and cybersecurity domains.
content:
arXiv:2603.12483v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Across many domains (e.g., IoT, observability, telecommunications, cybersecurity), there is an emerging adoption of conversational data analysis agents that enable users to "talk to your data" to extract insights. Such data analysis agents operate on timeseries data models; e.g., measurements from sensors or events monitoring user clicks and actions in product analytics. We evaluate 6 popular data analysis agents (both open-source and proprietary) on domain-specific data and query types, and find that they fail on stateful and incident-specific queries. We observe two key expressivity gaps in existing evals: domain-customized datasets and domain-specific query types. To enable practitioners in such domains to generate customized and expressive evals for such timeseries data agents, we present AgentFuel. AgentFuel helps domain experts quickly create customized evals to perform end-to-end functional tests. We show that AgentFuel's benchmarks expose key directions for improvement in existing data agent frameworks. We also present anecdotal evidence that using AgentFuel can improve agent performance (e.g., with GEPA). AgentFuel benchmarks are available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/RockfishData/TimeSeriesAgentEvals.

zh.title: 使用AgentFuel为时间序列数据分析代理生成表达性和可定制的评估
zh.summary: AgentFuel是一个新工具，使领域专家能够为处理时间序列数据的对话数据分析代理创建自定义的评估基准。该框架通过关注物联网、可观测性和网络安全领域中的有状态和特定事件查询，解决了现有评估中的空白。

fr.title: Générer des évaluations expressives et personnalisables pour les agents d'analyse de données de séries chronologiques avec AgentFuel
fr.summary: AgentFuel est un nouvel outil qui permet aux experts du domaine de créer des benchmarks d'évaluation personnalisés pour les agents d'analyse de données conversationnels traitant les données de séries chronologiques. Le cadre comble les lacunes dans les évaluations existantes en se concentrant sur les requêtes avec état et spécifiques aux incidents dans les domaines de l'IoT, de l'observabilité et de la cybersécurité.

de.title: Generierung ausdrucksstarker und anpassbarer Auswertungen für Zeitreihendaten-Analyse-Agenten mit AgentFuel
de.summary: AgentFuel ist ein neues Tool, das Domänenexperten ermöglicht, benutzerdefinierte Evaluierungsbenchmarks für konversationelle Datenanalyse-Agenten zu erstellen, die Zeitreihendaten verarbeiten. Das Framework schließt Lücken in bestehenden Evaluierungen, indem es sich auf zustandsbehaftete und incident-spezifische Abfragen in den Bereichen IoT, Observability und Cybersicherheit konzentriert.

es.title: Generación de evaluaciones expresivas y personalizables para agentes de análisis de datos de series temporales con AgentFuel
es.summary: AgentFuel es una nueva herramienta que permite a los expertos de dominio crear evaluaciones personalizadas para agentes de análisis de datos conversacionales que manejan datos de series temporales. El marco aborda brechas en evaluaciones existentes enfocándose en consultas con estado e incidentes específicos en los dominios de IoT, observabilidad y ciberseguridad.

title: AI Planning Framework for LLM-Based Web Agents
summary: Researchers formalized web task planning for LLM agents by mapping modern architectures to classical planning paradigms (BFS, Best-First Search, DFS), revealing systematic differences in reasoning quality. The work introduces evaluation metrics beyond success rates to better assess agent planning strategies.
content:
arXiv:2603.12710v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Developing autonomous agents for web-based tasks is a core challenge in AI. While Large Language Model (LLM) agents can interpret complex user requests, they often operate as black boxes, making it difficult to diagnose why they fail or how they plan. This paper addresses this gap by formally treating web tasks as sequential decision-making processes. We introduce a taxonomy that maps modern agent architectures to traditional planning paradigms: Step-by-Step agents to Breadth-First Search (BFS), Tree Search agents to Best-First Tree Search, and Full-Plan-in-Advance agents to Depth-First Search (DFS). This framework allows for a principled diagnosis of system failures like context drift and incoherent task decomposition. To evaluate these behaviors, we propose five novel evaluation metrics that assess trajectory quality beyond simple success rates. We support this analysis with a new dataset of 794 human-labeled trajectories from the WebArena benchmark. Finally, we validate our evaluation framework by comparing a baseline Step-by-Step agent against a novel Full-Plan-in-Advance implementation. Our results reveal that while the Step-by-Step agent aligns more closely with human gold trajectories (38% overall success), the Full-Plan-in-Advance agent excels in technical measures such as element accuracy (89%), demonstrating the necessity of our proposed metrics for selecting appropriate agent architectures based on specific application constraints.

zh.title: 用于基于LLM的网络代理的AI规划框架
zh.summary: 研究人员通过将现代架构映射到经典规划范式（BFS、最佳优先搜索、DFS），为LLM代理的网络任务规划形式化，揭示了推理质量的系统差异。该工作引入了超越成功率的评估指标，以更好地评估代理规划策略。

fr.title: Cadre de planification IA pour agents web basés sur LLM
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont formalisé la planification de tâches web pour les agents LLM en mappant les architectures modernes aux paradigmes de planification classiques (BFS, Best-First Search, DFS), révélant des différences systématiques dans la qualité du raisonnement. Le travail introduit des métriques d'évaluation au-delà des taux de succès pour mieux évaluer les stratégies de planification des agents.

de.title: KI-Planungsrahmen für LLM-basierte Web-Agenten
de.summary: Forscher formalisieren die Webaufgabenplanung für LLM-Agenten, indem sie moderne Architekturen auf klassische Planungsparadigmen (BFS, Best-First-Suche, DFS) abbilden und systematische Unterschiede in der Reasoning-Qualität offenbaren. Die Arbeit führt Bewertungsmetriken über Erfolgsquoten hinaus ein, um Agenten-Planungsstrategien besser zu bewerten.

es.title: Marco de planificación IA para agentes web basados en LLM
es.summary: Los investigadores formalizaron la planificación de tareas web para agentes LLM mapeando arquitecturas modernas a paradigmas de planificación clásicos (BFS, Best-First Search, DFS), revelando diferencias sistemáticas en la calidad del razonamiento. El trabajo introduce métricas de evaluación más allá de las tasas de éxito para evaluar mejor las estrategias de planificación de agentes.

title: On Using Machine Learning to Early Detect Catastrophic Failures in Marine Diesel Engines
summary: A machine learning approach using Random Forest can detect impending marine diesel engine failures by analyzing derivatives of sensor deviations rather than absolute values. The method enables operators to shut down engines before catastrophic damage, with validation on real-world engine data.
content:
arXiv:2603.12733v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Catastrophic failures of marine engines imply severe loss of functionality and destroy or damage the systems irreversibly. Being sudden and often unpredictable events, they pose a severe threat to navigation, crew, and passengers. The abrupt nature makes early detection the only effective countermeasure. However, research has concentrated on modeling the gradual degradation of components, with limited attention to sudden and anomalous phenomena. This work proposes a new method for early detection of catastrophic failures. Based on real data from a failed engine, the approach evaluates the derivatives of the deviation between actual sensor readings and expected values of engine variables. Predictions are obtained by a Random Forest, which is the most suitable Machine Learning algorithm among the tested ones. Traditional methods focus on deviations of monitored signals, whereas the proposed approach employs the derivatives of the deviations to provide earlier indications of abnormal dynamics, and to alert that a rapid and dangerous event is breaking out within the system. The method allows the detection of anomalies before measurements reach critical thresholds and alarms are triggered, which is the common method in industry. Consequently, operators can be warned in advance and shut down the engine, then prevent damage and unexpected power loss. Moreover, they have the time to safely change the ship route and avoid potential obstacles. Simulation results conf irm the effectiveness of the proposed approach in anticipating occurrence of catastrophic failures. Validation on real-world data further reinforces the robustness and practical applicability of the method. It is worth noting that data acquisition to train the predictive algorithm is not a problem, since a Deep Learning-based data augmentation procedure is used.

zh.title: 关于使用机器学习提前检测海洋柴油发动机灾难性故障
zh.summary: 一种使用随机森林的机器学习方法可以通过分析传感器偏差的导数而不是绝对值来检测即将发生的海洋柴油发动机故障。该方法使操作员能够在灾难性损害发生前关闭发动机，并在实际发动机数据上进行了验证。

fr.title: Sur l'utilisation du machine learning pour détecter précocement les défaillances catastrophiques des moteurs diesel marins
fr.summary: Une approche de machine learning utilisant Random Forest peut détecter les défaillances imminentes des moteurs diesel marins en analysant les dérivées des écarts de capteurs plutôt que les valeurs absolues. La méthode permet aux opérateurs d'arrêter les moteurs avant les dommages catastrophiques, avec validation sur des données réelles de moteur.

de.title: Über die Verwendung von maschinellem Lernen zur frühzeitigen Erkennung katastrophaler Ausfälle von Schiffsdieselmotoren
de.summary: Ein Machine-Learning-Ansatz mit Random Forest kann bevorstehende Ausfälle von Schiffsdieselmotoren erkennen, indem er Ableitungen von Sensorabweichungen statt Absolutwerte analysiert. Die Methode ermöglicht es Bedienern, Motoren vor katastrophalen Schäden abzuschalten, mit Validierung an realen Motordaten.

es.title: Sobre el uso del aprendizaje automático para detectar tempranamente fallas catastróficas en motores diésel marinos
es.summary: Un enfoque de aprendizaje automático que utiliza Random Forest puede detectar fallos inminentes de motores diésel marinos analizando derivadas de desviaciones de sensores en lugar de valores absolutos. El método permite a los operadores apagar los motores antes del daño catastrófico, con validación en datos reales del motor.

title: ToolTree: Efficient LLM Agent Tool Planning via Dual-Feedback Monte Carlo Tree Search and Bidirectional Pruning
summary: ToolTree is a Monte Carlo tree search-based planning framework for LLM agents that improves multi-step tool usage through dual-stage evaluation and bidirectional pruning. The approach achieves approximately 10% performance improvements over greedy tool selection across multiple benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.12740v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly applied to complex, multi-step tasks that require interaction with diverse external tools across various domains. However, current LLM agent tool planning methods typically rely on greedy, reactive tool selection strategies that lack foresight and fail to account for inter-tool dependencies. In this paper, we present ToolTree, a novel Monte Carlo tree search-inspired planning paradigm for tool planning. ToolTree explores possible tool usage trajectories using a dual-stage LLM evaluation and bidirectional pruning mechanism that enables the agent to make informed, adaptive decisions over extended tool-use sequences while pruning less promising branches before and after the tool execution. Empirical evaluations across both open-set and closed-set tool planning tasks on 4 benchmarks demonstrate that ToolTree consistently improves performance while keeping the highest efficiency, achieving an average gain of around 10\% compared to the state-of-the-art planning paradigm.

zh.title: ToolTree：通过双反馈蒙特卡洛树搜索和双向修剪实现高效的LLM代理工具规划
zh.summary: ToolTree是一个基于蒙特卡洛树搜索的规划框架，用于LLM代理，通过双阶段评估和双向修剪改进了多步工具使用。该方法相比贪心工具选择在多个基准测试中实现了大约10%的性能改进。

fr.title: ToolTree : Planification efficace des outils pour agents LLM via Recherche d'arbre de Monte Carlo à double rétroaction et élagage bidirectionnel
fr.summary: ToolTree est un cadre de planification basé sur la recherche d'arbre de Monte Carlo pour les agents LLM qui améliore l'utilisation d'outils multi-étapes grâce à l'évaluation double et à l'élagage bidirectionnel. L'approche réalise environ 10 % d'améliorations de performances par rapport à la sélection d'outils gloutons sur plusieurs benchmarks.

de.title: ToolTree: Effiziente LLM-Agent-Tool-Planung durch duale Feedback-Monte-Carlo-Baumsuche und bidirektionales Pruning
de.summary: ToolTree ist ein auf Monte-Carlo-Baumsuche basierendes Planungsrahmenwerk für LLM-Agenten, das die mehrstufige Tool-Nutzung durch duale Bewertung und bidirektionales Pruning verbessert. Der Ansatz erzielt etwa 10% Leistungsverbesserungen gegenüber gieriger Tool-Auswahl über mehrere Benchmarks hinweg.

es.title: ToolTree: Planificación eficiente de herramientas para agentes LLM mediante búsqueda de árbol de Monte Carlo de retroalimentación dual y poda bidireccional
es.summary: ToolTree es un marco de planificación basado en la búsqueda de árbol de Monte Carlo para agentes LLM que mejora el uso de herramientas de múltiples pasos a través de evaluación de dos etapas y poda bidireccional. El enfoque logra aproximadamente 10% de mejoras de rendimiento sobre la selección codiciosa de herramientas en múltiples benchmarks.

title: AI Model Modulation with Logits Redistribution
summary: AIM (AI Model Modulation) enables a single neural network to exhibit diverse behaviors for different tasks through logits redistribution without retraining. The paradigm supports both utility modulation for quality control and focus modulation for feature selection across image classification, segmentation, and text generation.
content:
arXiv:2603.12755v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large-scale models are typically adapted to meet the diverse requirements of model owners and users. However, maintaining multiple specialized versions of the model is inefficient. In response, we propose AIM, a novel model modulation paradigm that enables a single model to exhibit diverse behaviors to meet the specific end requirements. AIM enables two key modulation modes: utility and focus modulations. The former provides model owners with dynamic control over output quality to deliver varying utility levels, and the latter offers users precise control to shift model's focused input features. AIM introduces a logits redistribution strategy that operates in a training data-agnostic and retraining-free manner. We establish a formal foundation to ensure AIM's regulation capability, based on the statistical properties of logits ordering via joint probability distributions. Our evaluation confirms AIM's practicality and versatility for Al model modulation, with tasks spanning image classification, semantic segmentation and text generation, and prevalent architectures including ResNet, SegFormer and Llama.

zh.title: AI模型调制与Logits重分配
zh.summary: AIM（AI模型调制）使单个神经网络能够通过Logits重分配在不同任务中展现多样行为，无需重新训练。该范式支持效用调制用于质量控制和焦点调制用于特征选择，适用于图像分类、分割和文本生成。

fr.title: Modulation de modèle IA avec redistribution de Logits
fr.summary: AIM (Modulation de modèle IA) permet à un seul réseau neuronal d'exhiber des comportements divers pour différentes tâches grâce à la redistribution des logits sans réentraînement. Le paradigme prend en charge la modulation d'utilité pour le contrôle de qualité et la modulation de focus pour la sélection de caractéristiques dans la classification d'images, la segmentation et la génération de texte.

de.title: AI-Modellmodulation mit Logits-Umverteilung
de.summary: AIM (AI-Modellmodulation) ermöglicht es einem einzelnen neuronalen Netzwerk, durch Logits-Umverteilung ohne Umschulung unterschiedliche Verhaltensweisen für verschiedene Aufgaben zu zeigen. Das Paradigma unterstützt sowohl die Utility-Modulation zur Qualitätskontrolle als auch die Focus-Modulation zur Merkmalsauswahl bei Bildklassifizierung, Segmentierung und Textgenerierung.

es.title: Modulación de modelo de IA con redistribución de Logits
es.summary: AIM (Modulación de modelo de IA) permite que una única red neuronal exhiba comportamientos diversos para diferentes tareas a través de la redistribución de logits sin reentrenamiento. El paradigma admite la modulación de utilidad para control de calidad y la modulación de enfoque para selección de características en clasificación de imágenes, segmentación y generación de texto.

title: Context is all you need: Towards autonomous model-based process design using agentic AI in flowsheet simulations
summary: An agentic AI framework leveraging Claude Opus and GitHub Copilot automates chemical process flowsheet modeling through decomposed reasoning and code generation. The system demonstrates effectiveness for complex processes including reaction/separation, distillation, and entrainer selection.
content:
arXiv:2603.12813v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic AI systems integrating large language models (LLMs) with reasoning and tooluse capabilities are transforming various domains - in particular, software development. In contrast, their application in chemical process flowsheet modelling remains largely unexplored. In this work, we present an agentic AI framework that delivers assistance in an industrial flowsheet simulation environment. To this end, we show the capabilities of GitHub Copilot (GitHub, Inc., 2026), when using state-of-the-art LLMs, such as Claude Opus 4.6 (Anthropic, PBC, 2026), to generate valid syntax for our in-house process modelling tool Chemasim using the technical documentation and a few commented examples as context. Based on this, we develop a multi-agent system that decomposes process development tasks with one agent solving the abstract problem using engineering knowledge and another agent implementing the solution as Chemasim code. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework for typical flowsheet modelling examples, including (i) a reaction/separation process, (ii) a pressure-swing distillation, and (iii) a heteroazeotropic distillation including entrainer selection. Along these lines, we discuss current limitations of the framework and outline future research directions to further enhance its capabilities.

zh.title: 上下文就是一切：使用代理AI在流程仿真中实现自主模型化工艺设计
zh.summary: 一个利用Claude Opus和GitHub Copilot的代理AI框架通过分解推理和代码生成自动化化学过程流程图建模。该系统展示了对包括反应/分离、蒸馏和助剂选择在内的复杂过程的有效性。

fr.title: Le contexte est tout ce dont vous avez besoin : vers la conception de processus autonome basée sur des modèles utilisant l'IA agentive dans les simulations de diagrammes de flux
fr.summary: Un cadre d'IA agentive exploitant Claude Opus et GitHub Copilot automatise la modélisation des diagrammes de flux de procédés chimiques par le raisonnement décomposé et la génération de code. Le système démontre son efficacité pour les processus complexes incluant réaction/séparation, distillation et sélection d'entraîneur.

de.title: Kontext ist alles, was Sie brauchen: Auf dem Weg zur autonomen modellgestützten Prozessgestaltung mit agentischer KI in Fließbild-Simulationen
de.summary: Ein Rahmen für agentische KI, der Claude Opus und GitHub Copilot nutzt, automatisiert die Modellierung chemischer Prozessflussdiagramme durch zerlegte Argumentation und Codegenerierung. Das System zeigt Effektivität für komplexe Prozesse, einschließlich Reaktion/Trennung, Destillation und Entrainer-Auswahl.

es.title: El contexto es todo lo que necesitas: Hacia el diseño de procesos autónomo basado en modelos utilizando IA agentiva en simulaciones de diagramas de flujo
es.summary: Un marco de IA agentiva que aprovecha Claude Opus y GitHub Copilot automatiza el modelado de diagramas de flujo de procesos químicos mediante razonamiento descompuesto y generación de código. El sistema demuestra eficacia para procesos complejos incluyendo reacción/separación, destilación y selección de entraidor.

title: ODRL Policy Comparison Through Normalisation
summary: Researchers developed normalization algorithms for ODRL (Open Digital Rights Language) policies that convert them into minimal comparable forms. The approach enables policy comparison and makes complex policies representable in simpler ODRL fragments, addressing the language's complexity barrier.
content:
arXiv:2603.12926v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The ODRL language has become the standard for representing policies and regulations for digital rights. However its complexity is a barrier to its usage, which has caused many related theoretical and practical works to focus on different, and not interoperable, fragments of ODRL. Moreover, semantically equivalent policies can be expressed in numerous different ways, which makes comparing them and processing them harder. Building on top of a recently defined semantics, we tackle these problems by proposing an approach that involves a parametrised normalisation of ODRL policies into its minimal components which reformulates policies with permissions and prohibitions into policies with permissions exclusively, and simplifies complex logic constraints into simple ones. We provide algorithms to compute a normal form for ODRL policies and simplifying numerical and symbolic constraints. We prove that these algorithms preserve the semantics of policies, and analyse the size complexity of the result, which is exponential on the number of attributes and linear on the number of unique values for these attributes. We show how this makes complex policies representable in more basic fragments of ODRL, and how it reduces the problem of policy comparison to the simpler problem of checking if two rules are identical.

zh.title: 通过规范化的ODRL策略比较
zh.summary: 研究人员为ODRL（开放数字权利语言）策略开发了规范化算法，将其转换为最小可比较的形式。该方法使策略比较成为可能，并使复杂策略能够用更简单的ODRL片段表示，解决了该语言的复杂性障碍。

fr.title: Comparaison de politiques ODRL par normalisation
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé des algorithmes de normalisation pour les politiques ODRL (Open Digital Rights Language) qui les convertissent en formes minimales comparables. L'approche permet la comparaison des politiques et rend les politiques complexes représentables dans des fragments ODRL plus simples, résolvant la barrière de complexité du langage.

de.title: ODRL-Richtlinienvergleich durch Normalisierung
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten Normalisierungsalgorithmen für ODRL-Richtlinien (Open Digital Rights Language), die diese in minimale vergleichbare Formen umwandeln. Der Ansatz ermöglicht den Vergleich von Richtlinien und macht komplexe Richtlinien in einfacheren ODRL-Fragmenten darstellbar, wodurch die Komplexitätsbarriere der Sprache behoben wird.

es.title: Comparación de políticas ODRL mediante normalización
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron algoritmos de normalización para políticas ODRL (Lenguaje de Derechos Digitales Abiertos) que las convierten en formas mínimas comparables. El enfoque permite la comparación de políticas y hace que las políticas complejas sean representables en fragmentos ODRL más simples, abordando la barrera de complejidad del lenguaje.

title: Efficient and Interpretable Multi-Agent LLM Routing via Ant Colony Optimization
summary: AMRO-S is a routing framework for multi-agent LLM systems that uses ant colony optimization principles with semantic conditioning to efficiently route queries. The system improves quality-cost trade-offs while providing transparent routing decisions without increasing inference latency.
content:
arXiv:2603.12933v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-driven Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) have demonstrated strong capability in complex reasoning and tool use, and heterogeneous agent pools further broaden the quality--cost trade-off space. Despite these advances, real-world deployment is often constrained by high inference cost, latency, and limited transparency, which hinders scalable and efficient routing. Existing routing strategies typically rely on expensive LLM-based selectors or static policies, and offer limited controllability for semantic-aware routing under dynamic loads and mixed intents, often resulting in unstable performance and inefficient resource utilization. To address these limitations, we propose AMRO-S, an efficient and interpretable routing framework for Multi-Agent Systems (MAS). AMRO-S models MAS routing as a semantic-conditioned path selection problem, enhancing routing performance through three key mechanisms: First, it leverages a supervised fine-tuned (SFT) small language model for intent inference, providing a low-overhead semantic interface for each query; second, it decomposes routing memory into task-specific pheromone specialists, reducing cross-task interference and optimizing path selection under mixed workloads; finally, it employs a quality-gated asynchronous update mechanism to decouple inference from learning, optimizing routing without increasing latency. Extensive experiments on five public benchmarks and high-concurrency stress tests demonstrate that AMRO-S consistently improves the quality--cost trade-off over strong routing baselines, while providing traceable routing evidence through structured pheromone patterns.

zh.title: 通过蚁群优化的高效可解释多智能体LLM路由
zh.summary: AMRO-S是多智能体LLM系统的路由框架，使用蚁群优化原理与语义调节来高效路由查询。该系统在改进质量成本权衡的同时提供透明的路由决策，不增加推理延迟。

fr.title: Routage LLM Multi-Agent Efficace et Interprétable via Optimisation par Colonie de Fourmis
fr.summary: AMRO-S est un cadre de routage pour les systèmes LLM multi-agents qui utilise les principes d'optimisation par colonie de fourmis avec un conditionnement sémantique pour acheminer efficacement les requêtes. Le système améliore les compromis qualité-coût tout en fournissant des décisions de routage transparentes sans augmenter la latence d'inférence.

de.title: Effizientes und interpretierbares Multi-Agent-LLM-Routing durch Ameisenkolonie-Optimierung
de.summary: AMRO-S ist ein Routing-Framework für Multi-Agent-LLM-Systeme, das Ameisenkolonie-Optimierungsprinzipien mit semantischer Konditionierung nutzt, um Abfragen effizient weiterzuleiten. Das System verbessert Qualitäts-Kosten-Kompromisse und bietet gleichzeitig transparente Routing-Entscheidungen ohne erhöhte Inferenzlatenz.

es.title: Enrutamiento LLM Multi-Agente Eficiente e Interpretable mediante Optimización por Colonia de Hormigas
es.summary: AMRO-S es un marco de enrutamiento para sistemas LLM multiagente que utiliza principios de optimización por colonia de hormigas con acondicionamiento semántico para enrutar consultas de manera eficiente. El sistema mejora los compromisos de calidad-costo al proporcionar decisiones de enrutamiento transparentes sin aumentar la latencia de inferencia.

title: Structured Distillation for Personalized Agent Memory: 11x Token Reduction with Retrieval Preservation
summary: A structured distillation method compresses long agent conversation histories into compact 38-token exchanges while preserving 96% retrieval quality, achieving 11x token reduction. This approach enables efficient long-context handling by fitting thousands of conversation exchanges within a single prompt.
content:
arXiv:2603.13017v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Long conversations with an AI agent create a simple problem for one user: the history is useful, but carrying it verbatim is expensive. We study personalized agent memory: one user's conversation history with an agent, distilled into a compact retrieval layer for later search. Each exchange is compressed into a compound object with four fields (exchange_core, specific_context, thematic room_assignments, and regex-extracted files_touched). The searchable distilled text averages 38 tokens per exchange. Applied to 4,182 conversations (14,340 exchanges) from 6 software engineering projects, the method reduces average exchange length from 371 to 38 tokens, yielding 11x compression. We evaluate whether personalized recall survives that compression using 201 recall-oriented queries, 107 configurations spanning 5 pure and 5 cross-layer search modes, and 5 LLM graders (214,519 consensus-graded query-result pairs). The best pure distilled configuration reaches 96% of the best verbatim MRR (0.717 vs 0.745). Results are mechanism-dependent. All 20 vector search configurations remain non-significant after Bonferroni correction, while all 20 BM25 configurations degrade significantly (effect sizes |d|=0.031-0.756). The best cross-layer setup slightly exceeds the best pure verbatim baseline (MRR 0.759). Structured distillation compresses single-user agent memory without uniformly sacrificing retrieval quality. At 1/11 the context cost, thousands of exchanges fit within a single prompt while the verbatim source remains available for drill-down. We release the implementation and analysis pipeline as open-source software.

zh.title: 个性化智能体记忆的结构化蒸馏：11倍令牌减少与检索保留
zh.summary: 一种结构化蒸馏方法将长智能体对话历史压缩为紧凑的38令牌交换，同时保留96%的检索质量，实现11倍令牌减少。这种方法通过将数千次对话交换融入单个提示中，实现了高效的长上下文处理。

fr.title: Distillation Structurée pour la Mémoire d'Agent Personnalisée: Réduction des Tokens de 11x avec Préservation de la Récupération
fr.summary: Une méthode de distillation structurée compresse l'historique long des conversations d'agents en échanges compacts de 38 tokens tout en préservant 96% de la qualité de récupération, réalisant une réduction de 11x des tokens. Cette approche permet un traitement efficace du contexte long en ajustant des milliers d'échanges de conversation dans une seule invite.

de.title: Strukturierte Destillation für personalisierte Agent-Speicher: 11x Token-Reduktion mit Abrufbeibehaltung
de.summary: Eine strukturierte Destillationsmethode komprimiert lange Agent-Konversationsverläufe in kompakte 38-Token-Austausche, während 96% der Abrufqualität erhalten bleiben und eine 11x Token-Reduktion erreicht wird. Dieser Ansatz ermöglicht eine effiziente Handhabung des langen Kontexts, indem Tausende von Gesprächsaustausche in eine einzelne Eingabeaufforderung passen.

es.title: Destilación Estructurada para Memoria de Agente Personalizada: Reducción de 11x de Tokens con Preservación de Recuperación
es.summary: Un método de destilación estructurada comprime los historiales de conversación largos del agente en intercambios compactos de 38 tokens mientras se preserva el 96% de la calidad de recuperación, logrando una reducción de 11x de tokens. Este enfoque permite el manejo eficiente del contexto largo al ajustar miles de intercambios de conversación dentro de una sola solicitud.

title: Beyond Final Answers: CRYSTAL Benchmark for Transparent Multimodal Reasoning Evaluation
summary: CRYSTAL is a diagnostic benchmark with 6,372 instances evaluating multimodal LLM reasoning through verifiable intermediate steps, revealing systematic failures invisible to accuracy metrics. The paper introduces Causal Process Reward training to improve reasoning step alignment without manual annotation.
content:
arXiv:2603.13099v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce **CRYSTAL** (*__C__lear __R__easoning via __Y__ielded __S__teps, __T__raceability and __L__ogic*), a diagnostic benchmark with 6,372 instances that evaluates multimodal reasoning through verifiable intermediate steps. We propose two complementary metrics: *Match F1*, which scores step-level precision and recall via semantic similarity matching, and *Ordered Match F1*, which further penalizes disordered reasoning chains. References are constructed through a Delphi-inspired pipeline where four independent MLLMs generate trajectories, aggregated via semantic clustering and validated through human quality gates. Evaluation of 20 MLLMs, including commercial frontier systems not used during benchmark construction, reveals systematic failures invisible to accuracy: universal cherry-picking (precision far exceeds recall), non-monotonic scaling trade-offs, and disordered reasoning where no competitive model preserves more than 60% of matched steps in correct order. Beyond evaluation, we propose the **Causal Process Reward (CPR)**, a multiplicative reward that couples answer correctness with step-level alignment, and **CPR-Curriculum**, which progressively increases reasoning difficulty during training. CPR-Curriculum achieves +32% Match F1 via GRPO where additive reward strategies fail, improving reasoning without manual step annotation.

zh.title: 超越最终答案：CRYSTAL多模态推理评估透明性基准
zh.summary: CRYSTAL是一个包含6,372个实例的诊断基准，通过可验证的中间步骤评估多模态LLM推理，揭示准确度指标看不见的系统性失败。该论文引入因果过程奖励训练来改进推理步骤对齐，无需手动标注。

fr.title: Au-Delà des Réponses Finales: CRYSTAL Benchmark pour l'Évaluation Transparente du Raisonnement Multimodal
fr.summary: CRYSTAL est un benchmark diagnostique avec 6,372 instances évaluant le raisonnement LLM multimodal par des étapes intermédiaires vérifiables, révélant les défaillances systématiques invisibles aux métriques de précision. L'article introduit l'entraînement Causal Process Reward pour améliorer l'alignement des étapes de raisonnement sans annotation manuelle.

de.title: Jenseits endgültiger Antworten: CRYSTAL-Benchmark für transparente Bewertung multimodalen Reasonings
de.summary: CRYSTAL ist ein diagnostischer Benchmark mit 6.372 Instanzen zur Bewertung des multimodalen LLM-Reasonings durch verifizierbare Zwischenschritte und offenbart systematische Ausfälle, die für Genauigkeitsmetriken unsichtbar sind. Das Papier führt Causal Process Reward-Training ein, um die Ausrichtung der Reasoning-Schritte ohne manuelle Anmerkungen zu verbessern.

es.title: Más Allá de las Respuestas Finales: Referencia CRYSTAL para Evaluación Transparente de Razonamiento Multimodal
es.summary: CRYSTAL es un referencia diagnóstica con 6,372 instancias que evalúa el razonamiento LLM multimodal a través de pasos intermedios verificables, revelando fallas sistemáticas invisibles para las métricas de precisión. El documento introduce entrenamiento Causal Process Reward para mejorar la alineación de pasos de razonamiento sin anotación manual.

title: Steve-Evolving: Open-World Embodied Self-Evolution via Fine-Grained Diagnosis and Dual-Track Knowledge Distillation
summary: Steve-Evolving is a self-evolving framework for embodied agents that couples fine-grained execution diagnosis with knowledge distillation to organize experience in long-horizon tasks. The system distills successful trajectories into reusable skills and failures into executable guardrails for continual improvement.
content:
arXiv:2603.13131v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Open-world embodied agents must solve long-horizon tasks where the main bottleneck is not single-step planning quality but how interaction experience is organized and evolved. To this end, we present Steve-Evolving, a non-parametric self-evolving framework that tightly couples fine-grained execution diagnosis with dual-track knowledge distillation in a closed loop. The method follows three phases: Experience Anchoring, Experience Distillation, and Knowledge-Driven Closed-Loop Control. In detail, Experience Anchoring solidifies each subgoal attempt into a structured experience tuple with a fixed schema (pre-state, action, diagnosis-result, and post-state) and organizes it in a three-tier experience space with multi-dimensional indices (e.g., condition signatures, spatial hashing, and semantic tags) plus rolling summarization for efficient and auditable recall. To ensure sufficient information density for attribution, the execution layer provides compositional diagnosis signals beyond binary outcomes, including state-difference summaries, enumerated failure causes, continuous indicators, and stagnation/loop detection. Moreover, successful trajectories of Experience Distillation are generalized into reusable skills with explicit preconditions and verification criteria, while failures are distilled into executable guardrails that capture root causes and forbid risky operations at both subgoal and task granularities. Besides, Knowledge-Driven Closed-Loop Control retrieved skills and guardrails are injected into an LLM planner, and diagnosis-triggered local replanning updates the active constraints online, forming a continual evolution process without any model parameter updates. Experiments on the long-horizon suite of Minecraft MCU demonstrate consistent improvements over static-retrieval baselines.

zh.title: Steve-Evolving：开放世界具身自我演化通过细粒度诊断和双轨知识蒸馏
zh.summary: Steve-Evolving是一个用于具身智能体的自我演化框架，将细粒度执行诊断与知识蒸馏相结合，以组织长期任务中的经验。该系统将成功的轨迹蒸馏为可重复使用的技能，将失败转化为可执行的护栏，以实现持续改进。

fr.title: Steve-Evolving : Auto-Évolution Incarnée en Monde Ouvert via Diagnostic à Grain Fin et Distillation de Connaissances à Double Voie
fr.summary: Steve-Evolving est un cadre auto-évolutif pour les agents incarnés qui couple un diagnostic d'exécution à grain fin avec la distillation de connaissances pour organiser l'expérience dans les tâches d'horizon long. Le système distille les trajectoires réussies en compétences réutilisables et les échecs en garde-fous exécutables pour une amélioration continue.

de.title: Steve-Evolving: Verkörperte Selbstentwicklung in der offenen Welt durch feingranulare Diagnose und duale Wissensdestillation
de.summary: Steve-Evolving ist ein selbstentwickelndes Framework für verkörperte Agenten, das feingranulare Ausführungsdiagnose mit Wissensdestillation kombiniert, um Erfahrungen in langfristigen Aufgaben zu organisieren. Das System destilliert erfolgreiche Trajektorien zu wiederverwendbaren Fähigkeiten und Fehlschläge zu ausführbaren Schutzmaßnahmen für kontinuierliche Verbesserung.

es.title: Steve-Evolving: Auto-Evolución Encarnada en Mundo Abierto mediante Diagnóstico Detallado y Destilación de Conocimiento de Doble Vía
es.summary: Steve-Evolving es un marco auto-evolutivo para agentes encarnados que combina diagnóstico de ejecución detallado con destilación de conocimiento para organizar la experiencia en tareas de largo horizonte. El sistema destila trayectorias exitosas en habilidades reutilizables y fallos en protecciones ejecutables para una mejora continua.

title: When Right Meets Wrong: Bilateral Context Conditioning with Reward-Confidence Correction for GRPO
summary: Bilateral Context Conditioning and Reward-Confidence Correction enhance Group Relative Policy Optimization by leveraging contrasts between correct and incorrect reasoning traces. The improvements require no additional sampling and demonstrate consistent gains on mathematical reasoning benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.13134v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has emerged as an effective method for training reasoning models. While it computes advantages based on group mean, GRPO treats each output as an independent sample during the optimization and overlooks a vital structural signal: the natural contrast between correct and incorrect solutions within the same group, thus ignoring the rich, comparative data that could be leveraged by explicitly pitting successful reasoning traces against failed ones. To capitalize on this, we present a contrastive reformulation of GRPO, showing that the GRPO objective implicitly maximizes the margin between the policy ratios of correct and incorrect samples. Building on this insight, we propose Bilateral Context Conditioning (BICC), a mechanism that allows the model to cross-reference successful and failed reasoning traces during the optimization, enabling a direct information flow across samples. We further introduce Reward-Confidence Correction (RCC) to stabilize training by dynamically adjusts the advantage baseline in GRPO using reward-confidence covariance derived from the first-order approximation of the variance-minimizing estimator. Both mechanisms require no additional sampling or auxiliary models and can be adapted to all GRPO variants. Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvements across comprehensive models and algorithms. Code is available at \href{https://github.com/Skylanding/BiCC}{https://github.com/Skylanding/BiCC}.

zh.title: 当对遇见错：GRPO的双向上下文条件化与奖励置信度修正
zh.summary: 双向上下文条件化和奖励置信度修正通过利用正确和不正确推理轨迹之间的对比来增强群体相对策略优化。这些改进不需要额外采样，在数学推理基准上展示了一致的收益。

fr.title: Quand le Correct Rencontre l'Incorrect : Conditionnement de Contexte Bilatéral avec Correction de Confiance de Récompense pour GRPO
fr.summary: Le Conditionnement de Contexte Bilatéral et la Correction de Confiance de Récompense améliorent l'Optimisation de Politique Relative de Groupe en exploitant les contrastes entre les traces de raisonnement correctes et incorrectes. Les améliorations ne nécessitent aucun échantillonnage supplémentaire et démontrent des gains constants sur les benchmarks de raisonnement mathématique.

de.title: Wenn Richtig auf Falsch Trifft: Bilaterale Kontextkonditionierung mit Belohnungsvertrauenskorrektur für GRPO
de.summary: Bilaterale Kontextkonditionierung und Belohnungsvertrauenskorrektur verbessern die Group-Relative-Policy-Optimierung durch Nutzung von Kontrasten zwischen korrekten und inkorrekten Reasoning-Spuren. Die Verbesserungen erfordern keine zusätzlichen Stichproben und zeigen konsistente Gewinne bei mathematischen Reasoning-Benchmarks.

es.title: Cuando lo Correcto se Encuentra con lo Incorrecto: Acondicionamiento de Contexto Bilateral con Corrección de Confianza de Recompensa para GRPO
es.summary: El Acondicionamiento de Contexto Bilateral y la Corrección de Confianza de Recompensa mejoran la Optimización de Política Relativa de Grupo aprovechando contrastes entre trazas de razonamiento correctas e incorrectas. Las mejoras no requieren muestreo adicional y demuestran ganancias consistentes en puntos de referencia de razonamiento matemático.

title: Developing and evaluating a chatbot to support maternal health care
summary: A maternal health chatbot system for India combines stage-aware triage, hybrid retrieval over clinical guidelines, and LLM-conditioned generation with multilingual support. Comprehensive evaluation across component and end-to-end testing with clinician validation demonstrates the necessity of defense-in-depth design for high-stakes healthcare deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.13168v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The ability to provide trustworthy maternal health information using phone-based chatbots can have a significant impact, particularly in low-resource settings where users have low health literacy and limited access to care. However, deploying such systems is technically challenging: user queries are short, underspecified, and code-mixed across languages, answers require regional context-specific grounding, and partial or missing symptom context makes safe routing decisions difficult.
  We present a chatbot for maternal health in India developed through a partnership between academic researchers, a health tech company, a public health nonprofit, and a hospital. The system combines (1) stage-aware triage, routing high-risk queries to expert templates, (2) hybrid retrieval over curated maternal/newborn guidelines, and (3) evidence-conditioned generation from an LLM. Our core contribution is an evaluation workflow for high-stakes deployment under limited expert supervision. Targeting both component-level and end-to-end testing, we introduce: (i) a labeled triage benchmark (N=150) achieving 86.7% emergency recall, explicitly reporting the missed-emergency vs. over-escalation trade-off; (ii) a synthetic multi-evidence retrieval benchmark (N=100) with chunk-level evidence labels; (iii) LLM-as-judge comparison on real queries (N=781) using clinician-codesigned criteria; and (iv) expert validation. Our findings show that trustworthy medical assistants in multilingual, noisy settings require defense-in-depth design paired with multi-method evaluation, rather than any single model and evaluation method choice.

zh.title: 开发和评估支持孕产妇保健的聊天机器人
zh.summary: 针对印度的孕产妇健康聊天机器人系统结合了阶段感知分诊、临床指南混合检索和具有多语言支持的大语言模型条件生成。通过对组件和端到端测试的全面评估以及临床医生验证，展示了防御深度设计对高风险医疗保健部署的必要性。

fr.title: Développement et évaluation d'un chatbot pour soutenir les soins de santé maternelle
fr.summary: Un système de chatbot de santé maternelle pour l'Inde combine le triage conscient de l'étape, la récupération hybride des directives cliniques et la génération conditionnée par LLM avec support multilingue. L'évaluation complète des tests de composants et end-to-end avec validation clinicienne démontre la nécessité d'une conception de défense en profondeur pour le déploiement de soins de santé à enjeux élevés.

de.title: Entwicklung und Bewertung eines Chatbots zur Unterstützung der Müttergesundheitspflege
de.summary: Ein Chatbot-System für Müttergesundheit für Indien kombiniert stufenbewusste Triage, Hybrid-Retrieval über klinische Richtlinien und LLM-bedingte Generierung mit mehrsprachiger Unterstützung. Umfassende Bewertung über Komponenten- und End-to-End-Tests mit klinischer Validierung demonstriert die Notwendigkeit einer Defense-in-Depth-Gestaltung für hochriskante Gesundheitsbereitstellung.

es.title: Desarrollo y evaluación de un chatbot para apoyar la atención de la salud materna
es.summary: Un sistema de chatbot de salud materna para India combina triaje consciente de etapas, recuperación híbrida sobre directrices clínicas y generación condicionada por LLM con soporte multilingüe. La evaluación integral en pruebas de componentes y end-to-end con validación clínica demuestra la necesidad de un diseño de defensa en profundidad para el despliegue de atención médica de alto riesgo.
